mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS signals monetary accommodation that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.04% as of 2023-01-15 reflects recent Fed pivot from tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, supporting corporate earnings and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100,000 persons predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets react to labor strength.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined demand signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to improved earnings expectations across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 473,513 million and HOUST at 1,092 thousand as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand rebound
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 58% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST increase, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets price in reduced corporate profitability and economic slowdown.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate earnings pressure from weakening demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2023-01-15, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%, real rates are negative, supporting equity valuations. This combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 243.79 and FEDFUNDS at 1.04% as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: negative real rates environment supporting risk assets
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 56% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly signals underlying economic momentum. Quarters with GDPC1 growth exceeding 2% annualized predict positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as markets price in sustained economic expansion and earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,872.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 58% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity utilization. When both decline simultaneously month-over-month, the combined signal indicates demand destruction and capacity underutilization, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate profit margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.16 and PAYEMS at 146,404 thousand as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized economic contraction or early recession
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 60% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
